scan.title: Scan Barcode
scan.instructions: Point your camera at a product barcode
scan.permissionMessage: We need camera access to scan barcodes
scan.permissionDenied: Camera permission was denied. Please enable it in your device settings.

product.title: Product Analysis
product.notFoundMessage: This barcode is not in the Open Food Facts database.
product.noIngredientsMessage: This product was found but has no ingredient list in the database. The FODMAP analysis cannot be performed.
product.ingredientsPlaceholder: Paste or type ingredients separated by commas...
product.rescanMessage: Go back to the scanner to scan this product again.
product.reintroGroupsHint: FODMAP groups affected by this product. Reintroduce one group at a time.
product.disclaimer: This analysis is for informational purposes only. Always consult your dietitian for personalized FODMAP advice.

override.hardCheeseNote: This is an aged cheese — lactose is broken down during the ripening process.
override.butterNote: Butter contains negligible lactose — it is separated during churning.
override.gheeNote: Ghee is clarified butter — all milk solids and lactose are removed.
override.soySauceNote: Soy sauce is fermented — fructans and GOS are broken down during the process.
override.tempehNote: Tempeh is fermented — fructans and GOS in soybeans are broken down during fermentation.

history.title: Scan History
history.emptyMessage: Scan a product to see it here
history.clearConfirm: Are you sure you want to clear all scan history?

settings.title: Settings
settings.aboutText: FODMAP Scanner helps you identify FODMAP content in food products by scanning barcodes. Data is sourced from Open Food Facts and published peer-reviewed FODMAP research.
settings.disclaimerText: This app is for informational purposes only and is not a substitute for professional medical or dietary advice. FODMAP tolerances vary between individuals. Always consult a qualified dietitian before making dietary changes.
settings.fodmapGuideIntro: FODMAP stands for Fermentable Oligosaccharides, Disaccharides, Monosaccharides And Polyols. These are short-chain carbohydrates that are poorly absorbed in the small intestine and can cause digestive discomfort in sensitive people.
settings.fodmapFructans: Fructans: Found in wheat, rye, onion, garlic, and artichokes. One of the most common FODMAP triggers.
settings.fodmapGOS: GOS (Galacto-oligosaccharides): Found in legumes (lentils, chickpeas, beans), cashews, and pistachios.
settings.fodmapLactose: Lactose: The sugar in dairy products like milk, soft cheese, yoghurt, and ice cream.
settings.fodmapFructose: Excess Fructose: Found in honey, apples, pears, mangoes, and high-fructose corn syrup.
settings.fodmapSorbitol: Sorbitol: A sugar alcohol found in apples, pears, stone fruits (peach, plum, cherry), and sugar-free products.
settings.fodmapMannitol: Mannitol: A sugar alcohol found in mushrooms, cauliflower, celery, and sugar-free sweets.
settings.fodmapSafeFoodsList: Rice, oats, potatoes, carrots, cucumbers, tomatoes, lettuce, bananas, oranges, grapes, hard cheeses, lactose-free dairy, eggs, meat, fish, tofu, olive oil, maple syrup, and most herbs and spices.
settings.fodmapHighFoodsList: Wheat bread, pasta, onion, garlic, milk, soft cheese, apples, pears, watermelon, honey, mushrooms, cauliflower, legumes, cashews, and sugar-free gum.
settings.fodmapTip: The low FODMAP diet is a 3-phase approach: elimination, reintroduction, and personalisation. Work with a dietitian for best results.

ocr.title: Scan Ingredients
ocr.hint: Take a photo of the ingredient list on the packaging
ocr.resultsSubtitle: FODMAP analysis based on scanned ingredient list
ocr.noTextMessage: Could not read any text from the image. Try again with better lighting and make sure the ingredient list is clearly visible.

onboarding.step1Text: Use your camera to scan product barcodes from your local supermarket.
onboarding.step2Text: See a detailed breakdown of FODMAP categories with traffic light indicators.
onboarding.step3Text: See detailed FODMAP category breakdowns for each ingredient.

chrome.scanSubtitle: Live barcode scan with OCR and manual fallback
chrome.historySubtitle: Recent decisions, scores, and scan patterns
chrome.searchSubtitle: Look up ingredients, categories, and serving guidance

scan.actionScanText: Point the camera at a barcode and jump straight into analysis.
scan.actionOcrText: Use OCR when a product is missing ingredient data.
scan.actionManualText: Paste an EAN code when scanning is awkward or unavailable.
scan.scannerBody: Keep the barcode inside the frame. Manual entry is one tap away.
scan.heroTitle: Scan faster and understand results sooner.
scan.heroBody: The new flow keeps scan, OCR, and manual lookup in one place so you do not bounce between dead-end states.
scan.manualHelp: Use this when the barcode is damaged or the camera cannot focus quickly.
scan.recentDecisionsBody: Pick up where you left off without leaving the scan tab.
scan.noRecentBody: Your next barcode scan will appear here with its last known score.

search.heroTitle: Look up single ingredients before you scan a full product.
search.heroBody: Search is now positioned as a fast reference tool for known triggers, serving limits, and category breakdowns.
search.helperTriggerText: Jump straight into common offenders like garlic, onion, or lactose.
search.helperServingText: See stored safe portions when the dataset includes them.

history.heroTitle: Revisit what you scanned and spot patterns faster.
history.heroBody: The history view now behaves like a decisions log instead of a plain archive.

settings.heroTitle: Personalize the app without burying the useful parts.
settings.heroBody: Settings now leads with language, care preferences, and preview tools. Heavy education stays collapsible.
settings.learnBody: Keep the guide collapsed until it is actually needed.

product.greenBodyHigh: Most identified ingredients sit comfortably in the low-FODMAP range.
product.greenBodyLow: The result leans low FODMAP, but ingredient coverage is only partial.
product.yellowBodyHigh: This product may work in smaller servings, depending on your tolerance.
product.yellowBodyLow: Moderate risk is visible, and ingredient confidence suggests caution before relying on the score.
product.redBodyHigh: Known high-FODMAP ingredients are present strongly enough that this is probably not a good fit.
product.redBodyLow: The result trends high FODMAP, but incomplete ingredient coverage means you should verify the label carefully.
product.decisionHowToReadBody: Higher scores suggest a cleaner low-FODMAP fit based on the identified ingredient list and known trigger stacking.
